     The Numbers Business Report

Hairspray (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $118,871,849Details
International Box Office $83,951,012
Worldwide Box Office $202,822,861
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $104,961,464
Total $104,961,464
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In 1960s Baltimore, dance-loving teen Tracy Turnblad auditions for a spot on “The Corny Collins Show” and wins. She becomes an overnight celebrity, a trendsetter in dance, fun and fashion. Perhaps her new status as a teen sensation is enough to topple Corny’s reigning dance queen and bring racial integration to the show.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$27,476,745 (23.1% of total gross)
Legs:4.33 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:58.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,121 opening theaters/3,121 max. theaters, 7.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $206,643,505

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 20th, 2007 (Wide) by New Line
International Releases: September 14th, 2007 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: November 20th, 2007 by New Line Home Video
November 19th, 2007 by Entertainment in Video
MPA Rating: PG for language, some suggestive content and momentary teen smoking
Running Time: 115 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Fat Suit, Genderbending Casting, Remake
Geographical setting: Baltimore, MD
Plot point: Cross-Dressing, Dancing
Social setting: TV Industry
Subgenre: Romantic Musical
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Musical
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: New Line Cinema, Ingenious Film Partners, Zadan/Meron
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In 1960s Baltimore, dance-loving teen Tracy Turnblad auditions for a spot on “The Corny Collins Show” and wins. She becomes an overnight celebrity, a trendsetter in dance, fun and fashion. Perhaps her new status as a teen sensation is enough to topple Corny’s reigning dance queen and bring racial integration to the show.
